english: Mil. — de-snyéd (de snyed) so many. — dé-lta (de lta), dé-ltar (de ltar) (-du, or -na) so, p'a ni dé-lta ma yin-te (pha ni de lta ma yin te) as it is not so with the father Stg.; dé-lta-bu (de lta bu) of that kind, quality, or manner, such, esp. in B. — de-dé (de de) = de (de), but more emphatic, exactly that; de-de-bzhín-no (de de bzhin no) yes, so it is! *dhén-da (dhen da), de* (*de) C. = dé-lta (de lta) etc. — dé-na (de na) therein, in that place, there, here. — dé-nas (de nas) from, thence, from that place; afterwards, then, at that time, very frq. — dé-pa (de pa), dé-ma (de ma) Cs. one of that place, sect, religion etc. — dé-bas (de bas) 1. after a comparative, than that; 2. also dé-bas-na (de bas na), dés-na (des na), des (des), therefore, consequently, now then (δή) B. frq. — dé-bo (de bo) = de (de) Cs. — dé-tsam (de tsam) so much; dé-tsam-na (de tsam na), dé-tsa-na (de tsa na), then, at that time. — dé-tsug (de tsug), W. gen. *dé-zug* (*de zug*), so, thus. — dé-bzhin(-du) (de bzhin (-du)) according to that, thus, so; frq. for it, dé-bzhin-du gnáng-ṅo (de bzhin du gnang ngo) he allowed it Dzl.; dé-bzhin ṅó-šes-nas (de bzhin ngo shes nas) perceiving it Glr. — de-bzhin-nyíd (de bzhin nyid) (तत्त्व) essence, Was. (272), identity (297), like čos-nyíd (chos nyid) and some other similar expressions, = stoṅ-pa-nyíd (stong pa nyid), Trigl. fol. 20. — dé-zug (de zug) = dé-tsug (de tsug). — dé-yaṅ (de yang), dé'aṅ (de'ang), 1. this, or that, too; he also. 2. namely, to wit, viz., preceding specifications and detailed statements, sometimes also after a gerund, in which case it cannot be rendered in English. — de-rag (de rag) directly, immediately Sch. — de-ráṅ (de rang) = de-kó-na (de kho na), de-ráṅ yin (de rang yin) that is just the thing! exactly! to be sure! col. — de-ríṅ (de ring) B. and C. to-day, de-ríṅ-gi (de ring gi) of this day. — dé-ru (de ru), der (der), 1. into that, thereinto, into that place, thither, that way. 2. in that, therein, in that place, there, frq. — dé-la (de la) to this, to that; in, on, or at this; thereat, therewith, thereto, thereon; about that, concerning that; thereof, therefore. — dé-las (de las) from, out of, from that; after a comparative and yzhan (gzhan), than that. — de-srid (de srid) to such a length of time.

also diṅ (ding), to-day, deṅ-nas (deng nas) from this day forward Mil.; deṅ pyin-čad (deng phyin chad) or čad (chad) Dzl. id.; deṅ-gi dus (deng gi dus) the present time or age; deṅ-saṅ (deng sang) to-day and to-morrow; now-a-days; deṅ-saṅ lhá-rje (deng sang lha rje) the physicians of the present day Wdn.; deṅ-dus smán-pa (deng dus sman pa) Lt. id.

pf. and imp. of ₒdeṅ-ba ('deng ba), to go, to go away; deṅs-pa (dengs pa) seems to be the same form: so-sói gnas-su deṅs (so so'i gnas su dengs) Mil., raṅ-sar deṅs-so (rang sar dengs so) Pth. they went each to his own place; nám-mk'ar deṅ (nam mkhar deng) Mil. prob. it melted away, dissolved into air; sór-mo-rnams deṅs mdzád-pa (sor mo rnams dengs mdzad pa) to turn the fingers upwards (?). Schr. deṅs-pa (dengs pa) to ascend.
poultice, cataplasm, applied to sores and inflamed parts of the body Sch.

tibetanized form of the Persian دفتر documents, records, catalogues, registers, lists, books; deb-tér-pa (deb ther pa), deb-tér-mk'an (deb ther mkhan) Cs. keeper of the archives or records, recorder, archivist, librarian; déb-k'aṅ (deb khang) chancery, government office Schr.; déb-yig (deb yig) cover, envelope, stitched book Sch.
(perh. Bu-nan), a small, narrow bridge, foot-bridge Lh.
one day, some future time, Dzl. frq.; deu ... deu ... (de'u ... de'u ...) now ... now, at one time ... at another time Mil. (Tar. 165, 18 is prob. an incorr. reading).
for dé-ru (de ru), esp. as adv., then, at that time; der zad (der zad), der bas (der bas) Cs. that is all, there is nothing more, finis.
1. instrum. of de (de); des čog (des chog) with that it is enough, that will do Sch. — 2. for dé-bas (de bas), v. de (de) comp:
Cs.: 'fine, brave, noble, chaste; a title'; occurs frq. in Dzl. as a commendable quality of women.

1. num. figure: 131. — 2. two, a pair, a couple, used only in counting, measuring etc.: źo do re (zho do re) two drams of each Med.; *tá-bag do* (*ta bag do*) W. two platefuls. — 3. this, Schr.: dó-yi dón-du (do yi don du); gen. only in do-núb (do nub) this evening, to-night Mil.; bdag do-núb saṅ-gi mi (bdag do nub sang gi mi) I, a man only for to-day and to-morrow Mil.; Cs. also do-źág (do zhag), do-
